gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Reentrancy in swapExactInput during bonding-curve-to-DEX migration allows bonding-curve buys after threshold, under-provisioning DEX pool
candidate.claimed_root_cause: No reentrancy guard on swapExactInput; _getSwapImplementation reads status from storage at re-entry time; _setTokenStatus is a plain SSTORE with no mutex; if PORTAL_TRADE_V2 makes an external call (e.g. token transfer to recipient) before committing _setTokenStatus(DEX), a re-entrant swapExactInput sees Tradable status and routes to the bonding-curve handler
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker receives tokens at bonding-curve price after migration is triggered; final circulatingSupply exceeds amount accounted for in migrated DEX liquidity, leaving DEX pool underfunded relative to outstanding tokens

live_preconditions.name: portal_trade_v2_makes_external_call_before_setTokenStatus_DEX
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: Decompile or obtain source for PORTAL_TRADE_V2 facet at the address stored in immutable slot of impl 0xeb6c62d1885c421797d0c494e693c90d4b54125a; inspect migration path call ordering relative to _setTokenStatus(DEX)

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Entry point has only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) guard; no reentrancy protection; delegates to implementation selected by _getSwapImplementation at call time

Routing decision reads state.status from storage at the moment of each call; if status is still Tradable during a re-entrant call, routes to PORTAL_TRADE_V2 (bonding-curve handler) instead of PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER
Plain SSTORE with no mutex or lock; if an external call in PORTAL_TRADE_V2 occurs before this write, re-entrant calls see the pre-migration status

Uses delegatecall; PORTAL_TRADE_V2 executes in Portal's storage context, meaning any external calls made by PORTAL_TRADE_V2 (e.g. token transfers to recipient) occur while Portal's reentrancy state is unguarded

source_code_basis.symbols_or_lines: grep for nonReentrant/ReentrancyGuard/_locked/_status/_notEntered in src/*.sol returns zero matches
source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Confirms absence of any reentrancy guard in the in-scope Portal source files

attacker_path.actor: unprivileged EOA controlling a contract that implements receive() or onERC20Received()
attacker_inputs: ExactInputParams with outputToken = target Tradable token, inputToken = native/quote, recipient = attacker contract, amountIn = exactly enough to push circulatingSupply >= dexSupplyThresh
call_sequence: 1. Attacker calls swapExactInput with threshold-crossing amountIn
2. Portal delegates to PORTAL_TRADE_V2 via delegatecall
3. PORTAL_TRADE_V2 detects circulatingSupply >= dexSupplyThresh, triggers migration path
4. [UNVERIFIED] PORTAL_TRADE_V2 transfers tokens to recipient (attacker contract) or makes another external call BEFORE calling _setTokenStatus(DEX)
5. Attacker contract re-enters swapExactInput with a small buy
6. _getSwapImplementation reads status = Tradable (not yet updated), routes to PORTAL_TRADE_V2
7. Re-entrant buy executes at bonding-curve price against pre-migration reserve
8. Outer call resumes, calls _setTokenStatus(DEX) and migrates liquidity calculated from pre-reentrant reserve; circulatingSupply is now higher than accounted for
state_after: DEX pool funded for dexSupplyThresh tokens but circulatingSupply is higher
attacker holds tokens purchased at bonding-curve price not covered by DEX pool liquidity

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: Launched tokens purchased at bonding-curve price after migration threshold is crossed; DEX pool underfunded relative to outstanding token supply
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Tokens received at bonding-curve price in re-entrant call; these tokens are not covered by the DEX pool liquidity provisioned during migration
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: DEX pool is underfunded; other token holders face insolvency when attempting to sell into the DEX pool; protocol migration proceeds are misaccounted
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: After the DEX threshold is crossed, no bonding-curve trades should occur; the invariant is that all outstanding tokens are covered by DEX pool liquidity at migration; re-entrant bonding-curve buy creates tokens not covered by the migrated liquidity

setup: 1. Fork BSC at latest block with Portal proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
2. Identify PORTAL_TRADE_V2 immutable address from impl 0xeb6c62d1885c421797d0c494e693c90d4b54125a
3. Obtain or decompile PORTAL_TRADE_V2 source to confirm external call ordering relative to _setTokenStatus(DEX)

4. If PORTAL_TRADE_V2 source unavailable: deploy mock PORTAL_TRADE_V2 stub that (a) checks circulatingSupply >= dexSupplyThresh, (b) transfers tokens to recipient triggering attacker callback, (c) then calls _setTokenStatus(DEX), (d) then calls migrator
5. Deploy attacker contract whose receive()/onERC20Received() re-enters swapExactInput with a small buy
6. Set token state to Tradable with circulatingSupply one unit below dexSupplyThresh using vm.store
transaction_sequence: attacker.attack() -> swapExactInput(threshold-crossing params) -> [PORTAL_TRADE_V2 migration path] -> token.transfer(attacker) -> attacker.receive() -> swapExactInput(small buy) -> [re-entrant PORTAL_TRADE_V2 bonding-curve buy] -> return -> [outer migration completes]
expected_assertions: If vulnerable: re-entrant swapExactInput succeeds; attacker.tokenBalance() > 0 after re-entrant call; final circulatingSupply > amount accounted for in DEX pool liquidity
If protected: re-entrant swapExactInput reverts with TokenNotTradable or reentrancy revert
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: Re-entrant call reverts OR attacker token balance after migration is fully covered by DEX pool liquidity OR PORTAL_TRADE_V2 calls _setTokenStatus(DEX) before any external call to attacker-controlled address

rejection_reason: Not rejected. Structural conditions confirmed in available source (no reentrancy guard, status-based routing, plain SSTORE). Verdict is NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF rather than HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE because the critical assumption — that PORTAL_TRADE_V2 makes an external call to an attacker-controlled address before committing _setTokenStatus(DEX) — cannot be verified: PORTAL_TRADE_V2 is an immutable facet whose source is not present in this repository (src/ contains only Portal.sol, PortalBase.sol, PortalCommon.sol, and interfaces). Additionally, live_state_supports_preconditions is false because no live token in Tradable status near dexSupplyThresh has been confirmed from the available live_context snapshot.
